The free version now doesn’t let you change your theme or anything else without a screen popping up to subscribe. The widget doesn’t match what you see when you click on it and I loved that feature because I used to favorite from there. I wish I could say I would be subscribing again someday, but because I basically lost everything I built in the past three years of subscribing, just because I can’t afford it is very disheartening. I’ve lost enough already and it may sound silly but this is the one thing I counted on every day.

data.author: badst11

data.title: Misleading Offer and Cost
data.content:
Like others, I downloaded this app with the idea of trying it out before deciding if it was worth my time. Upon opening for the first time, there was about 10 minutes worth of questions I had to answer. As a customer who is curious about the product, getting barraged with personal questions before I can see what this product even is is already a big negative. Like most other companies on the internet, I assume they’re mining my data almost immediately (which is NOT a good feeling). 
Then my biggest problem is the subscription situation. After all those questions I get hit that it’s $70 for the year. There was no explanation on what that entailed (and again I hadn’t even made it to the interface of the actual app yet) and no other subscription options (which turns out there are?!?). They’re lucky I was still curious so I continued, thinking I will annoyingly have to make more time in my schedule just to cancel the subscription, again having to promise my time to something I don’t even know about. Inside the app, I find it mostly interesting and functional. The buttons on the Home Screen are a little confusing on what takes you where. 
Then I go to the subscription section to see what’s going on, thinking I’m going to cancel because $70 is too much. Doing that through Apple brought me to a page with the other options… finally. There are 4 options, none of which have any real explanation of what they are or do other than how much you’re charged per month/year. I notice a $35/year option….what does that entail? Why was that not presented to me in the beginning? How is that different $70? I literally have no way of knowing what that option actually comes with. 
Ultimately the interface is beautiful, the idea is great and truly almost there. But the elusiveness and lack of transparency around billing and subscriptions leaves a very bad taste. On to find a similar app without all the upfront hassle.
data.author: Msdetective

data.author: Royalty 12

data.title: Tons of updates & improvements, and Developers are on top of improving glitches fast!
data.content:
Currently there’s a Wealth of improvements going on in this app making it fun and enjoyable for numerous age ranges and numerous people’s needs. The app has beautiful graphic images and much more readability in the text on the images for those who have lower vision. There’s numerous ways to customize this app to fit your needs which I truly enjoy. I write my own affirmations and do my own customized image design in the app. The app now includes an AI affirmations writer for those who have things they want to work on AI will customize 10-16 affirmations per day if needed for you. It’s actually a very fun and useful tool. 


Unfortunately the last 2 updates have a little glitch that the developers are working on right now. With all the beautiful new features they have added. And a wonderful new event experience for mental health month…the ability to edit your own affirmations or the words of pre-written affirmations has disappeared from the app. Also the ability to edit your own categories has disappeared. 

But the app developers are aware of this little glitch which actually had numerous other hiccups 3 weeks ago. The developers are indeed on it. They really do care about the apps they create for us to use. They have been in very close contact with me, and they have been fixing all the little hiccups. 

Trust me when I say that what seems like a tiny annoyance to us is actually a huge problem for app developers. They have to go through numerous changes and make sure that the app not only functions and does what they want it to do and it doesn’t do something that they don’t want. But then they have to ensure that it’s coding and protocols meet all the rules of every single platform and operating system that can use the app. It’s really a lot of work for the app developers. And for them to be this on top of it. 

Definitely deserves a ton of credit. 4 out of 5 stars only because there’s the little glitch that the developers are working on as I’m typing. 

Will definitely be a 5-7stars which they don’t allow more than 5 stars but they should. For this app. Because they should allow the 2 stars for customer support so like other things 5 stars if everything is good 6 stars if customer service included with professionalism. 7 stars if treated with respect and made to feel important and valued. But you know only penthouses and restaurants get this star rating. Not apps. But when the apps catch up to the ratings they deserve this would be one of those 7 stars. Because they have a great development team. 

Princess
data.author: Pretty Deaf Princess

data.title: The Struggle
data.content: You can’t be positive for an app that refuses to want to assist in bettering “you”!! You lose everything! They always want a fee for an affirmation! Scratch the trial! It’s ridiculous. The app on the IPad does not load or look the same on your phone. Nothing on my phone is on my IPad. I have deleted the app 3xs. And it’s still not what it supposed to be to be.
data.author: BeautyBeatz

data.title: Love the app
data.content: Love the app but I can’t seem to sync the app on my phone to the app on my iPad
data.author: Nomadic01

data.title: Overpriced
data.content: I've been using this app for years and have even subscribed in the past; it's gotten way too expensive and is ridiculously overpriced along with affirmations that make absolutely no sense 👎
data.author: Prinofdisas

data.title: App Doesn’t Work
data.content: I purchased this app a few days ago and had a great time with it the first 3 or 4, but then it suddenly stopped working. It gives me affirmations, but does not allow me to customize the background or the themes. I even upgraded and paid a higher price thinking what I paid had limits but it’s doing the same thing, so now I’ve paid twice for a non-working app.
data.author: GB326

data.title: Was good, now bad and getting worse
data.content: I’ve been an avid user of the I am and the motivational app … the lifetime subscription that you may have paid once to get access to all themes is gone now. It no longer applies and you will have to start a new membership, which they only do on yearly monthly or weekly basis’ and are way too expensive in my opinion. Now it’s purely AI the tips are very irrelevant. It’s half of the time just quoting viral memes now? You try and contact customer support because there’s glitches and everything that they do there is AI as well. There’s no real people to talk to. And then they wonder why maybe not listening to customers feedback or being able to receive customer feedback has lessened their apps quality over the years. Hoping they actually hear some of this and change it otherwise we will have to move on from them as a thing of the past.
